enforced2026_detail: ABAWD time-limit enforcement resumes STATEWIDE in Illinois with the first potential countable month of February 2026. IDHS's prior statewide ABAWD waiver remained in place (per a federal court decision) through 01/31/2026; the first countable non-compliant month is February 2026, so the standard 3-month time-limit clock (Feb-Apr 2026) first results in benefit loss in May 2026 for non-compliant ABAWDs (per MR#26.03, item=174187, and Illinois Legal Aid). Under OBBBA/HR1 (P.L. 119-21, signed July 4, 2025) the ABAWD age range is expanded to 18-64 (exempt in the month of the 65th birthday) and area-based time-limit waivers (the former >10% / 'lack of sufficient jobs' standard) are eliminated. An earlier IDHS bulletin (item=175082) referenced a November 2025 waiver end / December 2025 first countable month, but the more recent MR#26.03 policy memo supersedes it with the court-extended 01/31/2026 waiver end and February 2026 first countable month. Net: enforced = yes (statewide), effective February 2026.
formulaDetail: Self-initiated community service / volunteering is counted toward the FLAT federal baseline of at least 80 hours per month (an average of 20 hours per week). IDHS MR#26.03 (item=174187) and the End-of-Waiver bulletin (item=175082) both state an ABAWD may 'do self-initiated Community Service with a community-based organization for an average of 20 hours per week (80 hours per month).' There is NO allotment-divided-by-minimum-wage formula governing general self-initiated nonprofit volunteering. Illinois DOES operate a separate, distinct 'Community Workfare' program whose assigned hours = household SNAP benefit amount / state minimum wage ($15.00), capped per the FLSA at total (Cash + SNAP) benefits / the larger of state or federal minimum wage. That allotment/min-wage formula applies ONLY to the assigned Community Workfare lane (verified on its own form, IL444-3673), NOT to the self-initiated community-service volunteer lane that any recipient can use at any community-based organization. Because general nonprofit volunteering is a clean standalone 80-hour lane with no provider registration, Illinois classifies as federal_80.
minimum_wage_note: Set to 0 because the self-initiated community-service (federal_80) lane uses a flat 80-hour requirement and NO minimum wage feeds that calculation. For reference only: the formula used in the SEPARATE assigned Community Workfare program uses the Illinois STATE minimum wage of $15.00/hr (effective 01/01/2025, unchanged for 2026; no further scheduled increases). IDHS uses the state minimum wage of $15.00 for the workfare formula, not local rates such as Chicago's $16.60/hr (effective 07/01/2025). If a future re-classification ever treated IL as workfare-formula, the applicable wage would be $15.00.
regional_wages_note: No sub-state regional minimum wage feeds the ABAWD/Community Workfare formula. IDHS uses the single state minimum wage ($15.00). Although Chicago ($16.60, 07/01/2025) and Cook County have higher local minimum wages, IDHS policy (WAG 25-06-08, item=19821) applies the state rate; these local wages do not create regional formula variation. Empty array.
qualifying_org_definition: Any community-based organization / nonprofit where the recipient performs self-initiated, unpaid community service or volunteering (examples cited by IDHS and Illinois Legal Aid: food pantry, community center, library, church). The organization must be able to verify the hours worked. No pre-approved list, MOU, or provider registration is required for the self-initiated community-service lane.
qualifyingOrgShort: Any community-based nonprofit organization (e.g., food pantry, community center, library, church) able to verify the volunteer's hours; no pre-approved list or provider registration required for the self-initiated lane.
verification_instrument: Form IL444-2610 (SNAP Activity Report) — the recipient enters the activity type/description and the date plus start/finish times for each session, signs and dates it, then gives it to an organization representative to enter the organization name and sign/date. The IL444-2610 is OPTIONAL: IDHS expressly accepts, as alternatives, (a) a signed letter or documentation from the community organization, or (b) a report from Serve Illinois - Galaxy Digital. (Distinct form: IL444-3673 'SNAP Work Requirement - Community Workfare Verification' is used for the SEPARATE assigned Community Workfare lane, not the self-initiated volunteer lane.)
verification_note: Confirmed via dedicated IDHS pages 'Reporting Volunteer/Community Service Hours' (item=178220), 'SNAP Work Requirements in MMC' (item=176167), and the live form PDF (IL444-2610.pdf). The self-initiated community-service lane uses IL444-2610; the assigned Community Workfare lane uses IL444-3673 (IES version only), per MR#26.03. A signed organization letter OR a Serve Illinois Galaxy Digital report are accepted equivalents.
submissionMechanism: Upload the completed IL444-2610 (or org letter / Serve Illinois Galaxy Digital report) via the Manage My Case (MMC) online portal 'Upload Documents' feature; OR mail/fax to the IDHS Central Scanning Unit (CSU), P.O. Box 19138, Springfield, IL 62794; OR bring/mail to the local Family Community Resource Center (FCRC).
submissionDeadline: No specific calendar deadline is published by IDHS for routine submission of volunteer hours. IDHS states recipients 'generally do not need to send proof to IDHS every month' (Illinois Legal Aid); hours are verified at application, redetermination, when an exemption is lost, and an ABAWD MUST report to IDHS when their work/volunteer hours fall below an average of 20 hours/week (80 hours/month). The ABAWD time-limit clock counts on a monthly basis (3 countable non-compliant months = loss of benefits), so practical compliance is monthly. No stand-alone fixed-date deadline for the IL444-2610 itself is stated.
submission_deadline: No specific calendar deadline published; report when hours drop below 80/month, and verify at application/redetermination. Time-limit clock is monthly (3 non-compliant months = ineligibility).
remote_note: No IDHS source (item=178220, item=174187, item=175082, item=177800), the IL444-2610 form, or Illinois Legal Aid addresses whether remote/virtual volunteering counts. Qualifying activity is defined by the nature of the organization (community-based / nonprofit) and verifiability of hours, not by location or modality. Silent.
accepts_outofstate_detail: No in-state registration, Illinois nexus, or geographic restriction on the certifying organization is found anywhere in IDHS policy (MR#26.03, item=178220, item=177800), the IL444-2610 form, or Illinois Legal Aid. Policy refers generically to 'a community-based organization' that can verify hours. Under the federal default (7 CFR 273.24), any qualifying nonprofit should be acceptable absent a state restriction; Illinois has not imposed one. Classified 'silent' (operationally permissive but not explicitly confirmed).
required_prestep_detail: No EDP, employment plan, MOU, county assignment, case-manager assignment, Serve Illinois registration, or workfare-site approval gates the self-initiated community-service lane. IDHS states recipients 'can use any job search method that works best for you' and may do 'self-initiated' community service. SNAP E&T participation is voluntary. Illinois's general SNAP 'work provisions' / work-registration obligation (ages 15-59, reported at application/redetermination) is a separate SNAP-wide requirement that does not gate the ABAWD volunteer lane. (Provider involvement / IL444-3673 is required ONLY for the distinct assigned Community Workfare lane, which is not the self-initiated path.)
geographic_waivers: None confirmed active for the post-February-2026 period. Illinois's prior statewide ABAWD time-limit waiver (court-extended) ended 01/31/2026, making February 2026 the first countable month. OBBBA/HR1 eliminated the former area-based (>10% unemployment / 'insufficient jobs') waiver standard, and all remaining FNS-approved ABAWD waivers terminate no later than November 2, 2026. As of mid-2026, no active Illinois ABAWD time-limit waiver (statewide or county-level, including the historically waivered Cook County / Chicago area) was confirmable in public FNS/FNA listings. Enforcement is statewide.
geographicWaivers: None confirmed active post-Feb-2026. Statewide waiver ended 01/31/2026 (court-extended); first countable month Feb 2026. OBBBA removed area-based waiver authority and terminates all remaining waivers by Nov 2, 2026. No active IL waiver (incl. historically waivered Cook County/Chicago) confirmable in FNS/FNA listings as of mid-2026. Statewide enforcement.
county_variation_note: IDHS administers SNAP statewide through Family Community Resource Centers under uniform state policy (MR#26.03). The self-initiated community-service lane, the IL444-2610 form, and the 80-hour rule are statewide and do not vary materially by county. (Geographic waivers, if any existed, would be the only source of locality variation; none are confirmed active.)
hours_formula_detail: Form/policy: 'an average of 20 hours per week (80 hours per month)' for self-initiated community service. Flat federal baseline; no workfare formula for general volunteering.
form_fields_note: IL444-2610 is an XFA-based fillable PDF (96 generically-named fields); field labels were corroborated from IDHS instructional pages (item=178220, item=176167) rather than the form's internal field names. Recipient fills activity type/description, date, and start/finish times, then signs/dates; the organization representative fills the organization name and signs/dates. No EIN or notarization indicated.
conflictsWithPrior: Prior file's verification_instrument named IL444-2610 as the sole/primary form and did not clearly identify IL444-3673. CORRECTION: IL444-2610 (SNAP Activity Report) is the correct form for the SELF-INITIATED community-service lane (confirmed). IL444-3673 ('SNAP Work Requirement - Community Workfare Verification', IES version only) is the form for the SEPARATE assigned Community Workfare lane (per MR#26.03, item=174187). The prior file's reliance on IL444-2610 for the self-initiated lane is correct; this record adds the IL444-3673 distinction.

Prior file cited an IL444-2610 PDF path '442610-202406_IES4-STA_RE.pdf'; the current live IDHS form URL is 'IL444-2610.pdf' (https://www.dhs.state.il.us/onenetlibrary/12/documents/Forms/IL444-2610.pdf). Source URL updated.
Prior file 'remote_stance' was 'unknown'; reclassified to 'silent' to match the required enum (no IL source addresses remote volunteering; meaning unchanged).

Prior file's geographic_waivers note stated the statewide waiver ended 'February 2026'; clarified to: the waiver ENDED 01/31/2026 and February 2026 is the first countable month (per MR#26.03). Substantively consistent.
Added top-level 'classification' = 'federal_80' (prior file used 'hours_formula' only). No change to the underlying determination — federal_80 confirmed.
unresolved: Exact internal field labels of the XFA-based IL444-2610 PDF could not be machine-extracted (generic field names only); labels inferred from IDHS instructional pages.
Whether remote/virtual volunteering counts toward the 80 hours is not addressed by any IL source (silent).
Whether an out-of-state 501(c)(3) may certify hours is not explicitly addressed by any IL source (silent; federal default likely permits).
No fixed calendar deadline for submitting the IL444-2610 is published (reporting is event/monthly-clock driven).

Whether any Illinois county/area holds a residual OBBBA-era ABAWD waiver in FY2026 could not be confirmed from a freshly fetched FNS/FNA county listing (the FNA waivers page timed out on fetch); search results indicate no active IL waiver, consistent with statewide enforcement from February 2026.
notes: VERIFICATION PASS (2026-06-22): Re-verified IL against live primary IDHS sources. CLASSIFICATION = federal_80 (confirmed, preserved from prior). Self-initiated community service / volunteering at any community-based organization is counted at a FLAT 80 hours/month (avg 20 hrs/week) per IDHS MR#26.03 (item=174187) and the End-of-Waiver bulletin (item=175082), both quoting 'self-initiated Community Service with a community-based organization for an average of 20 hours per week (80 hours per month).' No allotment/min-wage formula governs general volunteering. Illinois DOES run a SEPARATE assigned 'Community Workfare' program where assigned hours = household SNAP benefit / state minimum wage ($15.00), FLSA-capped — but that formula applies ONLY to the assigned workfare lane (verified on IL444-3673), NOT to the self-initiated volunteer lane any recipient can self-start at any nonprofit. This two-lane structure is why IL is federal_80, not workfare_formula or workfare_if_registered: the formula is not an option a general volunteer opts into, and general nonprofit volunteering needs no provider registration. Verification form for the self-initiated lane = IL444-2610 (SNAP Activity Report); a signed org letter OR a Serve Illinois Galaxy Digital report are accepted equivalents (item=178220, item=176167). Submission via Manage My Case upload, mail/fax to Central Scanning Unit, or local FCRC. Enforcement resumes STATEWIDE February 2026 (first countable month) after the court-extended statewide waiver ended 01/31/2026; OBBBA expanded the age range to 18-64 and removed area-based waivers; all remaining waivers terminate by Nov 2, 2026. No active IL geographic waiver confirmable. county_variation = false (uniform statewide IDHS administration). Genuine unknowns: remote stance (silent), out-of-state nonprofit (silent), no fixed form deadline, XFA form field labels not machine-extractable. CONFIDENCE = HIGH: primary IDHS policy sources directly confirm classification (federal_80) + 80-hour requirement + form (IL444-2610) + statewide Feb-2026 enforcement; only peripheral operational details remain unresolved.
